description:
You are acting as a deployment-engineer who has just rolled out a three-day batch of nightly updates.  
    All raw deployment logs already exist under  
      /home/user/deployment/logs/  
    and you must turn those raw logs into two human-readable reports that management will immediately ingest into an automated dashboard.

    1. SOURCE LOG STRUCTURE  
       a. There are exactly three log files:  
          • /home/user/deployment/logs/2023-09-01.log  
          • /home/user/deployment/logs/2023-09-02.log  
          • /home/user/deployment/logs/2023-09-03.log  

       b. Every line in those files follows this precise pattern (single space between fields):  
          YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS [server-name] STATUS: OK|FAIL  
          Example line:  
          2023-09-01 02:15:12 [web-01] STATUS: OK

    2. WHAT YOU MUST CREATE  
       Create a new directory  
          /home/user/deployment/reports  
       then generate TWO new files inside it, with the exact names, ordering and formatting shown below.  
       • /home/user/deployment/reports/deployment_failures_summary.csv  
       • /home/user/deployment/reports/daily_failure_counts.txt  

    3. deployment_failures_summary.csv – REQUIRED FORMAT  
       • Comma-separated, no extra spaces.  
       • First line is the header exactly as written:  
           Date,Server,Time  
       • Subsequent lines list ONLY the failed deployments (STATUS: FAIL).  
       • Lines must be sorted first by Date (ascending) and then by Time (ascending).  
       • Date must appear in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD (same as in the logs).  
       • Time must be the exact HH:MM:SS time portion from the log line.  

       The resulting file must therefore contain exactly five lines (one header + 4 data lines) and look **identical** to:

       Date,Server,Time
       2023-09-01,db-01,02:16:05
       2023-09-01,auth-01,02:18:20
       2023-09-03,db-03,02:15:02
       2023-09-03,cache-03,02:16:58

    4. daily_failure_counts.txt – REQUIRED FORMAT  
       • Plain text.  
       • One line per date found in the three log files, even if there were zero failures that day.  
       • Lines **must** be in ascending date order.  
       • Format of every line (including zero-count days):  
           YYYY-MM-DD: <number_of_failures>  

       The finished file must contain exactly these three lines:

       2023-09-01: 2
       2023-09-02: 0
       2023-09-03: 2

    5. OTHER CONSTRAINTS  
       • You do NOT need root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  
       • Do not alter the original log files.  
       • Use only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities available in a typical minimal distribution (awk, sed, grep, sort, cut, mkdir, etc.).  
       • Make sure the two output files are world-readable (at least 0644) so that the dashboard service can pick them up.  
       • Do not include any additional columns, blank lines, or trailing spaces in either report.  
       • When you are finished, you should be able to display the contents of both new files in the terminal and they must exactly match the samples shown above—character-for-character.  

    The automated evaluation will directly read those two files to verify:  
       1. The directory /home/user/deployment/reports exists.  
       2. The CSV has the correct header, four data rows, and the precise field order/content.  
       3. The TXT file lists all three dates in the correct order with the correct counts.  
       4. File permissions are 0644 or more permissive.  
       5. No extra files are present in /home/user/deployment/reports.

    Proceed with the necessary shell commands to complete the task.
